preview_text:
The model makes Canada a strong favorite at 73%, assigning Bosnia and Herzegovina just a 5% chance of victory. This 68-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast. The underdog's win probability sits well below the draw outcome at 22%.

The W/D/L spread suggests Canada is expected to control the match, though a draw remains a meaningful possibility at roughly one in five outcomes. The model sees Bosnia and Herzegovina as unlikely to prevail outright, implying the contest leans toward either a Canadian victory or a stalemate rather than a competitive three-way race.

Model and sportsbook diverge meaningfully here. The market prices Canada at 53%, draw at 26%, and Bosnia and Herzegovina at 21%—a 20-percentage-point gap favoring the underdog compared to the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment is more accurate. No Polymarket contract has yet been posted for this fixture.
